title: Convert CSV file to TSV (tab-separated values) using Python stdlib
description: Converts comma-separated values (CSV) to tab-separated values (TSV) using Python's standard library csv module.
explanation: The command reads CSV data from stdin, parses it with csv.reader (default delimiter is comma), and writes it to stdout using csv.writer with tab delimiter. It preserves quoting and escaping rules.
author: Marcos de Carvalho

examples.command: cat data.csv | python3 -c "import sys,csv; csv.writer(sys.stdout, delimiter='\t').writerows(csv.reader(sys.stdin))" | head -20

output:
col1	col2	col3
value1	value2	value3
notes: Handles quoted fields with commas correctly
Works with any CSV dialect compatible with Python's csv module
warnings: Large files may consume memory as all rows are read into list
Input must be valid CSV format
